title: Starship Scenes
description: Array of saved SF2e cinematic starship scene templates

x-player-visibility._summary: The runner has a separate Player View (shareable URL, often projected on a TV at the table). Some fields render to the player view as-is — DO NOT PUT SPOILERS IN THOSE FIELDS.
PLAYER_VISIBLE: additionalObjectives[]   ← every string is visible to players verbatim
availableRoles[]   ← role ids; the runner renders them as title-cased pills

starshipActions[*].outcomes.{criticalSuccess, success, failure, criticalFailure}   ← all four outcome strings render fully on the player card. ANY narrative spoiler placed here will be seen by the table.
GM_ONLY: starship.bonuses (skill bonuses on the PC ship)
threats[*]   ← entire threat data is GM-only; player view does not render threats
threats[*].routine and all routine actions (DCs, attack bonuses, damage, etc.)
starshipActions[*].dc / damage / isAttack / proficiency / traits / skills
AUTHORING_RULES: 1. Use a codename in starship.name and starshipActions outcome strings if the antagonist's identity is meant to be a reveal. Example: 'The Voice on the Channel' rather than 'Civusdaemon'. Tag the true identity in the scene-level description (GM-only).
2. roleDescriptions are read at the table — keep them flavorful, not story-spoilery.
3. additionalObjectives are visible — phrase them as the players' immediate goals, not the GM's reveal beats.
4. Outcome text in starshipActions is fully visible. NEVER reference unrevealed antagonists, twists, plot reveals, or GM-only mechanics in those strings.

5. Threats are entirely GM-only, so threat names CAN be the antagonist's true name without leaking. (But the runner shows threat data on the GM screen — if that screen is ever shared, treat threats as visible too.)
6. Story and reveal beats belong in a separate GM prep doc, not in the YAML.

x-import-info.notes: Scenes with matching IDs will be replaced on import. Each scene includes starship stats, threats with routines, role assignments, and victory conditions.

name.description: [GM-ONLY] Scene name shown in the GM's saved-scenes sidebar. Players see the running scene by ship name, not scene name.

level.description: [GM-ONLY] Used for DC/XP scaling; not rendered to players.

description.description: [GM-ONLY] Scene-level prep notes for the GM. Safe to include the antagonist's true identity, reveal timeline, plot beats, etc. — players never see this string.

vpRequired.description: [GM-ONLY] Hidden from player view; the inline VP chip is GM-side only.

customCondition.description: [GM-ONLY] The custom-condition text only renders on the GM screen.

availableRoles.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE] Role ids PCs can occupy. Standard set per GM Core p.231: 'captain', 'engineer', 'gunner', 'magic_officer', 'pilot', 'science_officer'. Repeats are allowed (e.g. ['gunner', 'gunner'] for two gunner slots). Custom 'niche' roles (per GM Core p.231 — e.g. a cook or corporate liaison) are also accepted: use any snake_case id. Unknown ids render as title-cased role pills (e.g. 'cook' → 'Cook') with a default gray color, and the runner falls back to a flat d20 for their initiative roll. Tag your custom role's actions with that same id in starshipActions[].role to wire them up.

additionalObjectives.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE] Each string renders verbatim on the player view's Objectives section. Phrase these as the players' in-fiction immediate goals (e.g. 'Recover the cargo manifest from the wreckage'), NOT as GM-only reveal beats or spoilers.

roleDescriptions.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE] Per-role flavor text rendered on each role card in the player view. Treat these as GM-spoken table flavor — keep them descriptive of the role, not predictive of the plot.

Starship.description: PC starship stat block. AC, Fort, Ref, and shield bonuses (e.g. 'Piloting': 2) are all clickable in the runner — clicking them rolls d20+bonus and logs to roll history.

name.description: Editable in setup mode and live during play.

fortitude.description: Fortitude save modifier — clickable to roll.

reflex.description: Reflex save modifier — clickable to roll.

shieldRegen.description: Shields regenerated at the start of each round (auto-applied via Next Round).

bonuses.description: Starship Bonuses per GM Core p.229 — item bonus to crew skill checks (e.g. {'Piloting': 2, 'Computers': 1}). Each chip is click-to-roll in the runner.

templateId.description: Optional — links this ship instance to a reusable SavedStarship template. When set and the template has isCampaignShip=true, end-of-scene HP/Shields persist back to the template for campaign continuity.

StarshipThreat.description: Enemy ship, hazard, or environmental threat. All numeric stats (Fort, Ref, Init, attack bonuses, DCs) are clickable in the runner — clicking rolls a d20+bonus or rolls damage and logs the result. Routine actions promote to top-level Attacks & Checks rows for one-tap rolling.

shieldRegen.description: Threat shields regenerate at start of each round, same as the PC ship.

fortitude.description: Click-to-roll Fortitude save in the runner.

reflex.description: Click-to-roll Reflex save in the runner.

initiativeSkill.description: e.g. 'Piloting', 'Arcana'. The skill rolled for initiative.

initiativeBonus.description: Modifier applied to the d20 initiative roll. Clickable to re-roll.

skills.description: Skill modifiers the threat has (e.g. {'Piloting': 12, 'Stealth': 8}). Used for any check the GM calls for outside the routine.

tacticalRole.description: GM tag for how to run this threat. 'complication' = penalizes crew unless addressed; 'indiscriminate' = hits everyone equally; 'standard' = targets the party ship directly.

routine.description: What the threat does each turn. Per GM Core, threats use preset routines instead of crew actions.

actionsPerTurn.description: Total action cost the threat spends per turn (3 max for most enemies).

description.description: Narrative summary of the threat's tactic, e.g. 'Scans then fires antimatter beam.'

variations.description: Optional alternate routines (per GM Core) triggered by specific scene conditions.

specialAbilities.description: Abilities outside the routine (per GM Core p.230 — e.g. auras, reactions). Rendered as a section on the threat card.

trigger.description: Optional — when the ability fires (e.g. 'When taking persistent damage').

abilities.description: Legacy: simple text-only ability tags. Prefer specialAbilities for new content.

immunities.description: Damage types or conditions the threat is immune to (e.g. ['mental', 'fire']).

routineActionsUsed.description: Runtime: action ids used this round (for budget tracking in the routine display).

ThreatRoutineAction.description: One action in a threat's routine. Attack rows render with click-to-roll attack bonus and click-to-roll damage badges; skill_check rows render with click-to-roll DC. Clicks no longer mark the action as 'used' — they just roll dice and log to history.

attackBonus.description: Required for type=attack to render the rollable +X chip.

damage.description: Damage expression (e.g. '2d10+5'). Rendered as a click-to-roll damage chip.

skill.description: Used for type=skill_check — the threat's skill rolled (e.g. 'Arcana').

vsDefense.description: Which target defense the action rolls against.

dc.description: Static DC for skill_check / ability actions. Click-to-roll in the routine card.

conditionalDamage.description: Bonus damage applied under specific conditions (described in conditionalEffect).

StarshipAction.description: Crew action available to PCs. Tagged by role (or 'any'). [PLAYER-VISIBLE] — every action renders in full on the player view's role cards, including all four outcome strings. Treat all string fields here as visible to the table. Damage chip is click-to-roll on the GM side; skill check rolls happen via the threat's defense the action targets, or the ship's bonus chips.

name.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE] Action name shown on the player's role card. Must match what the player sees on any printed cards/tables. Avoid spoilery references.

role.description: [GM-ONLY internal routing] Allowed role(s). Values: 'any' OR a role id, OR pipe-separated role ids (e.g. 'pilot|gunner', 'magic_officer|science_officer'). Standard roles: captain, engineer, gunner, magic_officer, pilot, science_officer. The runner uses this to group actions on the right role card; players see the result, not the value.

skills.description: [GM-ONLY] Skills the rolling PC can use (e.g. ['Piloting', 'Acrobatics']). Hidden from the player view (which already knows the role).

dc.description: [GM-ONLY] Static DC. If omitted, falls back to the scene's level-based DC. Player view does not show DCs.

description.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE] Short flavor text rendered on the player's role card. NEVER reference unrevealed antagonists, plot reveals, or GM-only mechanics here.

outcomes.description: [PLAYER-VISIBLE — ALL FIELDS] All four outcome strings render in full on the player's role card under labels Crit / Pass / Fail / Fumble. ANY narrative spoiler placed in any of these strings (e.g. naming the antagonist, referencing a hidden objective, foreshadowing a twist) will be seen by the table. Keep outcomes mechanical and in-fiction-immediate.

isAttack.description: True for gunner Strikes — adds the ATK badge in the runner.

proficiency.description: Required weapon proficiency for attack actions.

damage.description: Damage dice. Renders as a click-to-roll chip.
